{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-55078", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-409", "name": "Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary

`POST /api/v2/files` converts zip uploads to tar in memory via `CreateTarFromZip`, which enforced a per-entry size limit but no aggregate limit on total decompressed output, writing to an unbounded in-memory buffer.

> **Note:** Exploitation requires authenticated file-upload access and the impact is limited to availability (denial of service).

### Impact

An authenticated user could upload a zip within the 100 MiB upload limit but containing many highly compressible entries whose decompressed size exhausted memory, crashing `coderd` before any RBAC check. Repeated requests could keep the service unavailable. This is a denial of service; it does not allow data disclosure or code execution.

### Patches

The fix adds a metadata preflight check that sums projected entry sizes and a streaming writer that enforces the aggregate limit during decompression.

The fix was backported to all supported release lines:

| Release line | Patched version |
|---|---|
| 2.34 | [v2.34.2](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.34.2) |
| 2.33 | [v2.33.8](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.33.8) |
| 2.32 | [v2.32.7](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.32.7) |
| 2.29 (ESR) | [v2.29.17](https://github.com/coder/coder/releases/tag/v2.29.17) |

### Workarounds

Restrict file-upload permissions to trusted users or place a reverse proxy with request-body size limits in front of `coderd`.

### Resources

- Fix: #25877

### Credits

Coder would like to thank Anthropic's Security Team (ANT-2026-22438) for independently disclosing this issue!", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00602, "percentile": 0.44591}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-06T21:06:45Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-55078"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-06T21:06:45Z", "references": ["https://github.com/coder/coder/security/advisories/GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f", "https://github.com/coder/coder/pull/25877", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/coder/coder/security-advisories/GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/coder/coder", "summary": "Coder: Zip upload decompression lacks aggregate size limit, enabling denial of service", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-06T21:06:47Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2mg2-p7r7-g27f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.34.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.34.0, < 2.34.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.33.8", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.33.0, < 2.33.8"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.32.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.30.0, < 2.32.7"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.29.17", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/coder/coder/v2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.17.0, < 2.29.17"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Coder: Zip upload decompression lacks aggregate size limit, enabling denial of service
